notes: The 'oi' is French /wa/, so the first syllable is PWAH, not POY: PWAH-sawn. Named after French mathematician Siméon Denis Poisson. Wiktionary gives General American /ˈpwɑ.sɒn/ (no vowel-length mark) and RP /ˈpwɑː.sɒn/ ~ /ˈpwɑː.sɒ̃/ (the ː length and nasalized ending are RP, not GA). The dominant developer/student error is the fully anglicized POY-sun rhyming with 'poison', which is why the term reliably trips people in stats, queueing theory, and event-rate modeling. Educated stats usage converges on PWAH-sawn, so consensus rather than truly contested.
